nodes.context: French-Canadian organized crime group of nine brothers operating in Montreal from 1950s to mid-1980s, involved in extortion, drug trafficking, loan sharking, and murder.
Mafia family in Montreal that rivaled the Dubois Brothers in the 1960s and 1970s.
Rival gang in Montreal that was wiped out by the Dubois Brothers.
Unnamed motorcycle gang out-muscled by the Dubois Brothers for drug market control.
Irish-Canadian gang specializing in truck hijackings and theft, operated in Montreal.
French biker gang controlling drug trade in Saint-Louis Square, Montreal, in early 1970s.
Biker gang hired by Dubois Brothers to fight Devil's Disciples, later managed drug sales.

edges.context: The Dubois Brothers rivaled the Cotroni family in the 1960s and 1970s, challenging their supremacy.
The Dubois Brothers wiped out the rival Irish gang to expand territory.
The Dubois Brothers out-muscled a motorcycle gang to take over the lucrative drug market.
McSweens joined as associates in 1957, selling stolen goods to Dubois in exchange for weapons and supplies.
War broke out in 1973 over territory; Dubois killed several McSween members and took over their territory.
Dubois hired Popeyes to fight Devil's Disciples over drug turf; fifteen Disciples killed and they were driven out.
Dubois hired Popeyes as muscle to fight Devil's Disciples; Popeyes later managed drug sales for Dubois.
